rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors¿ (on behalf of their insured, defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.) motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability, and survival) and granted as to issue 4 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and issues 1, 2 and 3 of summary adjudication, Ms. Sultz¿s depositon testimony, Rich-Tex, Inc.¿s responses to general order 129 interrogatories and Mr. Watkins¿ testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Montgomery was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (joint compound) attributable to Rich-Tex. As to whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Montgomery¿s risk of developing mesothelioma, Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Intervenors did not satisfy their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Montgomery¿s exposure to asbestos-containing Rich-Tex joint compound was a substantial factor. Intervenors¿ failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that exposure attributable to Rich-Tex was a substantial factor precludes Intervenors from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Intervenors also did not satisfy their burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims against them. Mr. Cohen¿s declaration fails to take into account much of Ms. Sultz¿s deposition testimony regarding Mr. Montgomery¿s work with Rich-Tex joint compound and is thus insufficient to allow defendant to sustain its burden. As to adjudication issue 4 (punitive damages), Intervenors sustained their initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether Rich-Tex acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Intervenors¿ objections to Ms. Sultz¿s deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MONROE AMEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Intervenors¿ (on behalf of their insured, defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.) motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages). Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Intervenors did not satisfy their burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to Rich-Tex, Inc.¿s special interrogatories and Rich-Tex, Inc.¿s responses to standard interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Intervenors did not satisfy their burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against Rich-Tex. When read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony, Mr. Watkins¿ declaration merely creates a triable issue whether plaintiff worked with a product attributable to Rich-Tex. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 4, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant York International Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. When read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony, Mr. Ziffer¿s declaration merely creates a triable issue whether plaintiff worked with a product attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. rulings.Case Title: CLIFFORD HOWARD VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 4, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at its request. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), denied as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff has dismissed his false representation cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: KENTON SHAIFFER et al VS. DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 4, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 10, Defendant Crosby Valve, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Shaiffer was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. Martin¿s declaration are sustained. The court did not consider plaintiffs¿ further response to defendant¿s special interrogatories. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
